headline: Damage is concentrated in Meta prospecting; CPMs are up from a transient competitor sale, but the controllable lever is creative.

channels.cpa_read: primary source of the blended CPA rise
channels.lever: ad-fatigue + auction CPM — per 'If CTR is low: ad fatigue -> refresh creative' and 'If CPM is high: high competition -> placements'
rising CPCs as competitors bid spring sales; quality score steady
channels.verdict: softening with the same external pressure; secondary
healthy — demand intact, so this is a paid-efficiency problem, not a demand collapse
channels.why: Brand search holding while prospecting deteriorates localises the problem to top-of-funnel paid, not the brand itself.

moves: Competitor B spring sale + new 'cooling' video angle
competitor_read.durability: TRANSIENT — spring sales lapse; the cooling-angle push is the more durable threat but targets the seasonal hot-sleeper job, not gift-intent
competitor_read.verdict: Do not torch budget defending the contested cooling angle during a competitor sale. Let CPMs normalise; compete where we're strong (gift-intent, durable creative).

implication: External CPMs explain part of the CPA rise but are not controllable this week. The controllable, highest-leverage lever the channel data points to is refreshing the fatigued prospecting creative.
source: ads / Platform Selection + Optimization lever trees + competitor-profiling / competitor move read (durability)
